course_description:
This industry placement course will be taken in the final year of the Master of Occupational Health and Safety Science and the Master of Occupational Hygiene/Master of Occupational Health and Safety (MOH/MOHSSc).

This course will provide real industry experience in an approved workplace. Students will be expected to be present in the workplace during normal working hours and undertake OHS related work as agreed between the workplace supervisor and the placement coordinator.

Over the duration of the placement it is anticipated that the student will be exposed to a range of OHS professional competencies and will reflect upon these in a work journal. For more information about enrolling in this course, visit science.uq.edu.au/placements .

Final year MOH/MOHSSc students will be required to undertake 240 hours within a nominated workplace, under the guidance of both a host workplace supervisor and the course coordinator. This work integrated learning experience provides an opportunity for students to apply the OHS knowledge, skills and capabilities gained during their university studies within the workplace.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Restricted to MOHSSc and dual MOH/MOHSSc students with permission to enrol. See course description for further information.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
This course involves 240 hours of OHS industry work at a host organisation which equates to two days per week for 15ﾠweeks (23 February 2026 to 5 June 2026).
An orientation session will be held at 10am on Wednesday 18th February 2026. This session is mandatory and will cover the course requirements and assessments.
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with an opportunity to integrate theoretical knowledge and skills by undertaking an industry placement with an approved host organisation.

learning_outcomes.description: Integrate and apply OHS knowledge within a work environment.
Acquire high level skills that will enhance the application of existing knowledge.
Plan strategies to address and solve complex OHS issues and associated change management.
Demonstrate the ability to effectively communicate orally and in writing with both workers, management, and other relevant stakeholders.
Develop a high level of competence in the application of OHS and related skills and practice.
Apply knowledge of ethics to OHS professional practice.
Appraise your own performance by reflective practice in order to achieve the best possible outcomes.

assessment_summary.title: OHS Competency Demonstration
assessment_summary.due_date: OHS Task 1: Written 13/03/2026 5:00 pm; OHS Task 2: Oral 25/03/2026; OHS Task 3: Written 17/04/2026 5:00 pm; OHS Task 4: Oral 29/04/2026; OHS Task 5: Written 15/05/2026 5:00 pm; OHS Task 6: Oral 27/05/2026

assessment_details.title: Learning Contract
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students are to develop a learning contract in consultation with their workplace supervisor that outlines a range of OHS tasks that the student will undertake during the placement. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly note any use of AI or MT. A failure to declare generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
This assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, and there is no system for applying "mark" penalties. If the assessment item is not submitted by the submission due date outlined in the course profile, it will be considered a non-submission, resulting in a "fail".

assessment_details.title: OHS Competency Demonstration
assessment_details.due_date: OHS Task 1: Written 13/03/2026 5:00 pm OHS Task 2: Oral 25/03/2026 OHS Task 3: Written 17/04/2026 5:00 pm OHS Task 4: Oral 29/04/2026 OHS Task 5: Written 15/05/2026 5:00 pm OHS Task 6: Oral 27/05/2026
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to undertake a range of OHS tasks that demonstrate their ability to apply OHS theoretical knowledge and technical/non-technical skills in practice. During the 240 hour placement students are to complete a range of OHS tasks and submit a written description of three (3) of these tasks and participate in three (3) one-on-one online discussions of three (3) of these tasks with the course coordinator. For each of the Written tasks, students are required to complete the OHS Task Form and upload supporting evidence to Turnitin. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly note any use of AI or MT. A failure to declare generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Written tasks: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Oral tasks: Each one-on-one discussion session will be allocated 15 minute on the relevant due date. Assigned times will be published on Blackboard.

assessment_details.title: Ethical Practice in OHS
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to choose four (4) values from the Australian Institute of Health and Safety and write a reflection on how they applied each of the four values into professional practice and any potential challenges faced in their application during the OHS Industry placement. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: UQ Orientation to industry placements An orientation session will be held at 10am on Wednesday 18th February. This session is mandatory and will cover the course requirements and assessments. Learning outcomes: L07

Host Organisation Orientation Students will spend Week 1 orientating themselves to their host workplace, including the undertaking of compulsory induction programs and developing a learning contract. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Revision week
learning_activities.topic: OHS Placement Activity Students spend weeks 2-15 undertaking their industry placement including completing OHS tasks as outlined in their learning contract. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Learning activities
